mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the housing market (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals a surge in consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 3 months signals a tightening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over the next 2 months signals a supply-side shock, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < -10% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in real disposable personal income (RSAFS) over the next 3 months, combined with a 2% increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over the same period, signals a surge in aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 5% and INDPRO > 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the next 2 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 1% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% increase in the employment rate (PAYEMS) over the next 4 months signals a strengthening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 0.2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the GDP (GDPC1) over the next 3 months signals a surge in aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 10% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
